title: Read Polybar Wi-Fi SSID over NetworkManager D-Bus
description: Replace scan-prone nmcli dev wifi SSID lookup in polybar-status with NetworkManager D-Bus reads: PrimaryConnection -> ActiveConnection Type/SpecificObject -> AccessPoint Ssid. This should preserve the SSID display without triggering active Wi-Fi scans from the status bar.
close_reason: Implemented NetworkManager D-Bus SSID lookup in the Rust Polybar helper. Verified cargo tests, right --once prints Mastiff, no real nmcli dev wifi process remains, and nix build succeeds.

title: Keep Logitech receiver out of TLP USB autosuspend
description: Mouse intermittently stopped after USB autosuspend. Live device 046d:c52b Logitech USB Receiver had power/control=auto and runtime_status=suspended despite the udev whitelist. Add TLP USB_DENYLIST plus a stricter usb_device udev rule, then verify /etc/tlp.conf and live /sys/bus/usb/devices/1-1/power/control=on after nrs.

title: Investigate boot default and rebuild slowness
description: Why: default boot generation unexpectedly starts GNOME and rebuilds feel slow even when cached. What: inspect boot loader defaults/specializations, make the menu preselect the top item if appropriate, and measure where rebuild time is spent.

title: Keep CPU frequency next to CPU load
description: Split CPU load out of the combined Polybar right status so modules-right can order cpuload cpufreq freqmenu status. Preserve dim foreground-alt rendering for 0% CPU cores and keep the Polybar native frequency menu anchored next to the frequency label.
close_reason: Implemented cpuload as a separate Rust-backed Polybar module ordered before cpufreq and freqmenu. Added a render test that 0% CPU cores use foreground-alt dimming. Verified cargo tests, nix build, nrs, generated Polybar config, service logs, and live helper processes.

title: Make herbstluftwm-dark the boot default specialization
description: Set systemd-boot loader.conf default to the newest generated nixos-generation-*-specialisation-herbstluftwm-dark.conf entry after boot entries are installed. Keep the base system and all specialisations intact; only change the bootloader default selection.
close_reason: Added systemd-boot install hook that selects the newest nixos-generation-*-specialisation-herbstluftwm-dark.conf entry. Verified /boot/loader/loader.conf defaults to generation 2063 herbstluftwm-dark after nrs.

title: Resolve deprecated Home Manager qt.platformTheme.name = gnome
description:
Build warning:
  evaluation warning: felix profile: The value `gnome` for option `qt.platformTheme.name` is deprecated. Use `adwaita` instead.

Current cause:
  Stylix Home Manager module sets:
    qt.platformTheme.name = config.stylix.targets.qt.platform;
  With current auto-selected platform "gnome", Home Manager emits the deprecation warning.

Related source:
  /nix/store/w6na21bl2lbllmnwiyxdlfv7gf26gn0v-source/modules/qt/hm.nix sets platformTheme.name from stylix.targets.qt.platform.
  /nix/store/h9wn92hv33sizprch7fcp16lfs1k3w5j-source/nixos/modules/config/qt.nix shows NixOS Qt platform themes now include adwaita/adwaita-dark style packages and documents platformTheme choices.

Options:
  1. If choosing Stylix-supported qtct, override stylix.targets.qt.platform = "qtct"; this should also avoid the deprecated gnome value.
  2. Try an explicit adwaita/adwaita-dark Qt platform/style override. Tradeoff: may fight Stylix, and pinned Stylix currently warns for any platform other than qtct.
  3. Keep gnome and accept the deprecation temporarily. Tradeoff: future Home Manager/Nixpkgs may remove it.

Decision needed:
  Same as the Stylix Qt task: choose supported qtct/Kvantum versus GNOME/Adwaita integration. Resolve that first, then verify both Qt warnings together with nix build.

title: Decide Qt theming path for Stylix: qtct vs GNOME/Adwaita
description:
Build warning:
  evaluation warning: felix profile: stylix: qt: `config.stylix.targets.qt.platform` other than 'qtct' are currently unsupported: gnome. Support may be added in the future.

Current value:
  nixos-option stylix.targets.qt.platform => "gnome"

Trace/source:
  Stylix nixos module /nix/store/w6na21bl2lbllmnwiyxdlfv7gf26gn0v-source/modules/qt/nixos.nix auto-selects "gnome" when GNOME is enabled and Plasma/LXQt are not. Stylix Home Manager module /nix/store/w6na21bl2lbllmnwiyxdlfv7gf26gn0v-source/modules/qt/hm.nix warns when the platform is not "qtct".

Options:
  1. Override stylix.targets.qt.platform = "qtct". This follows Stylix support, likely uses qtct/Kvantum config, and should remove this warning. Tradeoff: Qt apps may no longer use GNOME platform integration.
  2. Keep Stylix auto-selected "gnome" for GNOME integration. Tradeoff: warning remains; Stylix says this path is unsupported and may break.
  3. Investigate whether Stylix upstream now supports an Adwaita platform path. Tradeoff: more research; pinned Stylix module currently only documents support for qtct and warns for gnome.

Recommendation:
  Prefer option 1 unless GNOME-native Qt dialog/style integration matters more than warning-free supported Stylix config.

title: Avoid deprecated prev.system in breezy-desktop overlay
description:
Build warning:
  evaluation warning: 'system' has been renamed to/replaced by 'stdenv.hostPlatform.system'

Corrected trace result after fixing Firefox pkgs.system:
  NIX_ABORT_ON_WARN=1 nix build --impure --no-link .#nixosConfigurations.gurke.config.system.build.toplevel --show-trace points at breezy-desktop input flake.nix:52:
    inherit (self.packages.${prev.system}) ...

Why it happens:
  The upstream breezy-desktop overlay uses the deprecated prev.system alias from nixpkgs.

Local fix implemented:
  Replace use of flake-inputs.breezy-desktop.overlays.default in modules/nixos/desktops/gnome.nix with a local equivalent overlay that uses prev.stdenv.hostPlatform.system.

Verification:
  Run NIX_ABORT_ON_WARN=1 nix build --impure --no-link .#nixosConfigurations.gurke.config.system.build.toplevel --show-trace again. Expected result: this warning should disappear and the next remaining warning should be one of the Stylix Qt warnings.
close_reason: Replaced breezy-desktop upstream overlay use with a local equivalent overlay using prev.stdenv.hostPlatform.system. Verified NIX_ABORT_ON_WARN now advances to the Stylix Qt warning instead of the system alias warning.

title: Place CPU frequency menu at frequency label
description: Split the CPU frequency display into its own Rust-backed Polybar module before freqmenu, and wake the status renderer from BlueZ D-Bus PropertiesChanged signals so Bluetooth enabled/disabled state appears immediately.
close_reason: Split CPU frequency into its own Rust-backed Polybar module before freqmenu, added cpu-freq --tail, and verified live Polybar loads status cpufreq freqmenu with the helper running.

title: Keep Logitech receiver out of USB autosuspend
description: The Logitech Unifying receiver 046d:c52b may stop responding after suspend when USB autosuspend is globally enabled. Add a targeted udev rule that keeps only that receiver at power/control=on.
close_reason: Added a targeted udev rule for Logitech Unifying receiver 046d:c52b so USB autosuspend remains global but the mouse dongle stays at power/control=on.

title: Build Rust Polybar status engine
description: Replace Polybar's noisy xwindow module and right-side polling shell modules with one Nix-packaged Rust helper that emits sanitized Polybar markup and reduces always-running child processes.
close_reason: Implemented polybar-status Rust helper, wired Polybar to it, verified build/switch/runtime

title: Reduce idle power wakeups from Polybar and Wi-Fi
description: Investigate expensive Polybar modules, replace the top-process implementation with a cheaper approach if appropriate, and enable Wi-Fi power saving through NixOS configuration.
close_reason: Replaced polybar-topprocess with a compiled procfs sampler and enabled NetworkManager Wi-Fi power save; remaining xwindow title warning filed separately.

title: Fix Polybar service stop timeout
description: During nrs on 2026-05-14, polybar-dark.service received SIGTERM but did not stop before systemd's timeout and was SIGKILLed after about 90s. Logs showed the service wrapper waiting on Polybar children. Investigate the launch script/service stop behavior so Polybar and polybar-status child processes exit promptly on restart without delaying switches.
close_reason: Added bounded Polybar wrapper shutdown: send TERM, wait up to about 2s, then KILL remaining Polybar pids. Verified nrs stopped polybar-dark.service immediately without timeout and restarted it cleanly.

title: Put Polybar clock after tray
description: Move date/time back to Polybar modules after the tray and make low-battery blinking cover both icon and remaining time.
close_reason: Implemented and verified tray/date/time ordering plus whole low-battery label blinking

title: Stop Polybar xwindow title warning spam
description: Polybar still logs unmatched braille spinner characters from the xwindow module when the active terminal title contains Codex spinner text such as '⠦ dotfiles'. Investigate a minimal fix, likely replacing internal/xwindow with a sanitized title helper or adjusting title updates/font coverage.
close_reason: Replaced internal/xwindow with sanitized polybar-status title helper
